llm.summary: The task is complete in substance. The repository has Prisma/SQLite wired through package dependencies, prisma.config.ts, migrations, a generated-client setup path, and scripts/db-prepare.mjs applying migrations, generating the client, and running idempotent seed defaults. The schema covers endpoints, immutable IDs, names, titles/descriptions, enabled/protected state, delete codes, ordered parameters, response cases, default responses, timestamps, and basic endpoint/case failure config fields. The seed creates one protected enabled echo endpoint with one parameter and two response cases, and the unit test exercises db:prepare twice against an isolated SQLite file. docs/generated/db-schema.md matches the implemented Prisma shape. The provided deterministic check summary shows npm run db:prepare, npm run test:unit, and npm run verify passing.
satisfied_exit_criteria: npm run db:prepare creates/updates SQLite runtime state via Prisma migrations, client generation, and seed execution.
db:prepare repeatability is covered by tests/unit/db-prepare.test.ts and the deterministic check output shows the command remains safe across repeated runs without duplicate seed rows.
Prisma schema represents endpoint names, descriptions, enabled state, ordered parameters, response cases, default responses, delete codes, timestamps, and basic failure config fields.
docs/generated/db-schema.md matches the current Endpoint, EndpointParam, and ResponseCase persistence shape and seed assumptions.
All required commands passed in the deterministic check summary: npm run db:prepare, npm run test:unit, and npm run verify.
The implementation maps to docs/product-specs/endpoint-tool-management.md and stays within the persistence-foundation scope.

The seed creates one protected enabled echo endpoint with one parameter and two response cases, and the unit test exercises db:prepare twice against an isolated SQLite file. docs/generated/db-schema.md matches the implemented Prisma shape.
